uiBuilder.label: Collapse / Expand Panel
uiBuilder.description: A fully custom collapsible panel: a clickable header with a chevron or +/- trigger that shows or hides its content. Stack several to build an accordion.

properties.name: headingText
properties.label: Heading
properties.description: The label shown in the header. Selecting the header toggles the content.

properties.name: bodyText
properties.label: Body text
properties.description: Fallback text shown in the content area when nothing is placed in the component's slot. Leave empty if you only want slotted content.
properties.defaultValue: Place any content here. In UI Builder you can drop other components into this panel's slot; this text shows when the slot is empty.

properties.name: expanded
properties.label: Expanded by default
properties.description: Whether the panel starts open when the page loads.

properties.name: triggerIcon
properties.label: Trigger icon
properties.description: The icon that indicates open/closed state.

properties.name: iconPosition
properties.label: Icon position
properties.description: Whether the trigger icon sits on the left or right of the heading.

properties.name: bordered
properties.label: Show border
properties.description: Optional. When on, the panel is wrapped in a rounded border/card. Off by default for the clean, flat look.

properties.name: disabled
properties.label: Disabled
properties.description: When on, the header can't be toggled.

properties.name: contentSizing
properties.label: Content sizing
properties.description: How the content area is sized. Grow to fit: the area grows with the content (use Content height as a minimum floor) — best for a Playbook. Fixed: exact height, scrolls inside.

properties.name: contentHeight
properties.label: Content height
properties.description: A CSS length, e.g. 60vh or 500px. In Grow to fit mode it's the MINIMUM height (the area grows taller as needed). In Fixed mode it's the exact height. Required when embedding a component that sizes to its parent (like a Playbook), which otherwise renders at ~0 height.
